55: **Curaçao**, level with one other team in **Group E** on 1 point and positions not yet separated, face **Côte d'Ivoire**, who sit 2nd in the group on 3 points. **Côte d'Ivoire** need at least a point to stay in the frame for progression, but **Curaçao** — knowing they need a win to have any hope — will come forward, making this a lively and open contest with real stakes at both ends.
56: **Ecuador**, level with one other team in **Group E** on 1 point with positions not yet separated, meet group leaders **Germany**, who sit top of **Group E** on 6 points with a +7 goal difference. **Germany** arrive already through and capable of rotating, yet a first-place finish is worth protecting — for **Ecuador**, only a win combined with the right result in the other **Group E** game keeps their knockout hopes alive.
57: **Japan**, level with one other team in **Group F** on 4 points and positions not yet separated, host 3rd-placed **Sweden**, who are on 3 points in the group. **Sweden** must win to stay in contention, giving them little choice but to push forward, while **Japan** know a draw may well be enough to confirm their place in the knockouts — a tense, chess-like battle that could hinge on a single moment.
58: **Tunisia**, rooted to the bottom of **Group F** on 0 points after two defeats, face **Netherlands**, who are level with one other team at the top of the group on 4 points with positions not yet separated. **Netherlands** will be eager to seal first place and secure the softer knockout route, while **Tunisia** have nothing to lose and everything to play for — which makes them dangerous despite the gulf in the standings.
59: **Türkiye**, bottom of **Group D** on 0 points after two defeats, face group leaders **USA**, who sit top of **Group D** on a perfect 6 points. **USA** are already through and likely have one eye on the knockouts, but maintaining their unbeaten record and goal difference matters — **Türkiye** arrive desperate to salvage pride and, with results elsewhere helping them, an unlikely lifeline.
60: **Paraguay**, level with one other team in **Group D** on 3 points and positions not yet separated, face **Australia**, who are equally level on 3 points in the group with positions not yet separated. One win, one exit — both sides know this is as close to a knockout match as the group stage gets, and expect an intense, physical contest where neither team can afford to blink first.
51: **Switzerland**, level with one other team at the top of **Group B** on 4 points and positions not yet separated, meet **Canada**, equally level on 4 points in the group and also unresolved. The winner almost certainly locks up first place and earns the easier knockout draw, while the loser is left sweating on the **Bosnia and Herzegovina**–**Qatar** result — two unbeaten sides, one huge prize, and no margin for hesitation.
52: **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, level with one other team in **Group B** on 1 point and positions not yet separated, face **Qatar**, who are equally unresolved on 1 point in the group. With the top two already pulling clear, both sides know a defeat here almost certainly ends their tournament — expect an attack-minded, open contest from the first whistle as both teams chase the win that keeps them mathematically alive.
49: **Scotland**, sitting 3rd in **Group C** on 3 points, face **Brazil**, who are level with one other team at the top of the group on 4 points with positions not yet separated. **Scotland** need a result to stay in contention, while **Brazil** arrive hungry to edge clear at the summit — a tactical battle where **Scotland**'s defensive resolve will be pushed to its absolute limit.
50: **Morocco**, level with one other team at the top of **Group C** on 4 points and positions not yet separated, face bottom side **Haiti**, who are on 0 points after two defeats. **Morocco** will be eager to confirm their place in the knockouts with a convincing win, and the margin of victory could yet prove decisive when the final **Group C** standings are calculated.
53: **Czechia**, level with one other team in **Group A** on 1 point and positions not yet separated, must overcome group leaders **Mexico**, who sit top of **Group A** on a commanding 6 points. **Mexico** are already through but a positive result protects their first-place finish and knockout momentum — for **Czechia**, only a win keeps their slim hopes of progression mathematically alive going into the final count.
54: **South Africa**, level with one other team in **Group A** on 1 point and positions not yet separated, face **Korea Republic**, who sit 2nd in the group on 3 points. **Korea** need at minimum a draw to confirm their place in the knockouts, while **South Africa** know that only a win — combined with the right result in the other **Group A** game — offers any realistic path through, ensuring desperation and intensity in equal measure.
